sentences.zh: 然后，OpenAI 似乎也需要苹果的大量专业知识来制造这个东西。

sentences.en: What do you make of that, that OpenAI needed hardware know-how from Apple?
sentences.zh: OpenAI 需要 Apple 提供硬件专业知识，您对此有何看法？

sentences.en: Or at least Apple alleges that OpenAI needed hardware know-how from Apple to make whatever the next thing is?
sentences.zh: 或者至少苹果声称 OpenAI 需要苹果的硬件知识来制造下一个产品？

blocks.html_en: To some extent, whether you’re talking about trade secrets or just regular legal aboveboard know-how from hiring their employees, of course they needed it. They’ve seen a ton of hardware in AI crash and burn and they’re trying to not become the next Humane AI Pin. It can be incredibly embarrassing to make an AI hardware device in this day and age. Chances are you’re probably going to fail miserably and become a laughingstock. And so, if you want to avoid that, of course you need to get the best people on your side.

sentences.en: To some extent, whether you’re talking about trade secrets or just regular legal aboveboard know-how from hiring their employees, of course they needed it.
sentences.zh: 在某种程度上，无论你是在谈论商业秘密，还是只是雇佣员工时获得的常规合法法律知识，他们当然需要它。

sentences.en: They’ve seen a ton of hardware in AI crash and burn and they’re trying to not become the next Humane AI Pin.
sentences.zh: 他们目睹了大量人工智能硬件的崩溃和烧毁，他们试图不成为下一个人性化的人工智能 Pin。

sentences.en: It can be incredibly embarrassing to make an AI hardware device in this day and age.
sentences.zh: 在当今时代，制造人工智能硬件设备可能会非常尴尬。

sentences.en: Chances are you’re probably going to fail miserably and become a laughingstock.
sentences.zh: 你很可能会惨败并成为笑柄。

sentences.en: And so, if you want to avoid that, of course you need to get the best people on your side.
sentences.zh: 因此，如果你想避免这种情况，你当然需要让最优秀的人才站在你这边。

blocks.html_en: Hardware is hard, as everyone says, so of course they want to get experts in there that have experience actually being incredibly successful at hardware. That’s not surprising to me and I think it’s a smart decision. OpenAI has demonstrated in the past — and this is typical of every tech company, it’s not just OpenAI— that sometimes they’re a little bit disconnected from what the consumer actually wants.

sentences.en: Hardware is hard, as everyone says, so of course they want to get experts in there that have experience actually being incredibly successful at hardware.
sentences.zh: 正如每个人都说的那样，硬件很难，所以他们当然希望聘请在硬件方面拥有令人难以置信的成功经验的专家。

sentences.en: That’s not surprising to me and I think it’s a smart decision.
sentences.zh: 这对我来说并不奇怪，我认为这是一个明智的决定。

sentences.en: OpenAI has demonstrated in the past — and this is typical of every tech company, it’s not just OpenAI— that sometimes they’re a little bit disconnected from what the consumer actually wants.
sentences.zh: OpenAI 过去已经证明——这是每家科技公司的典型特征，不仅仅是 OpenAI——有时它们与消费者的实际需求有些脱节。

blocks.html_en: You roll something out, no one really wants it. Okay. With software that’s a little easier to pivot from, as we’ve seen from OpenAI deleting its side quests and stuff. They’re like, “Oh, we’re not doing this anymore. We’re not doing this anymore. We’re rolling this into that.” With hardware, you can’t do that. You’d better have it right from the get-go.

sentences.en: You roll something out, no one really wants it.
sentences.zh: 你推出一些东西，没有人真正想要它。

sentences.en: Okay.
sentences.zh: 好的。

sentences.en: With software that’s a little easier to pivot from, as we’ve seen from OpenAI deleting its side quests and stuff.
sentences.zh: 使用更容易转向的软件，正如我们从 OpenAI 中看到的那样，删除了其支线任务和内容。

sentences.en: They’re like, “Oh, we’re not doing this anymore.
sentences.zh: 他们会说：“哦，我们不再这样做了。

sentences.en: We’re not doing this anymore.
sentences.zh: 我们不再这样做了。

sentences.en: We’re rolling this into that.”
sentences.zh: 我们正在把这个变成那个。”

sentences.en: With hardware, you can’t do that.
sentences.zh: 有了硬件，你就无法做到这一点。

sentences.en: You’d better have it right from the get-go.
sentences.zh: 你最好从一开始就拥有它。

blocks.html_en: I’m glad you said consumer, because that brings me to what I think is the heart of the matter for OpenAI, which is that it was going to be the consumer AI company, Anthropic famously focuses on enterprise and it’s been lucrative for them; maybe that’s where all of the money is. But OpenAI was going to take down Google, maybe through Bing, maybe through its own app. It was going to be a consumer company.

sentences.en: I’m glad you said consumer, because that brings me to what I think is the heart of the matter for OpenAI, which is that it was going to be the consumer AI company, Anthropic famously focuses on enterprise and it’s been lucrative for them; maybe that’s where all of the money is.
sentences.zh: 我很高兴你说消费者，因为这让我想到了 OpenAI 问题的核心，那就是它将成为消费者人工智能公司，Anthropic 众所周知专注于企业，这对他们来说是有利可图的；也许这就是所有钱的所在。

sentences.en: But OpenAI was going to take down Google, maybe through Bing, maybe through its own app.
sentences.zh: 但 OpenAI 可能会通过 Bing，也可能通过它自己的应用程序来击败 Google。

sentences.en: It was going to be a consumer company.
sentences.zh: 它将成为一家消费品公司。

blocks.html_en: The only reason you would make hardware is to go attack the consumer, to go attack the iPhone. So, OpenAI’s ambitions, at least as I could see them, were first to take market share from Google, which is one of the most lucrative businesses in the history of the world, and then to go attack the iPhone, which is the most famous and culturally dominant tech product that has ever existed.

sentences.en: The only reason you would make hardware is to go attack the consumer, to go attack the iPhone.
sentences.zh: 你制造硬件的唯一原因是去攻击消费者，去攻击 iPhone。

sentences.en: So, OpenAI’s ambitions, at least as I could see them, were first to take market share from Google, which is one of the most lucrative businesses in the history of the world, and then to go attack the iPhone, which is the most famous and culturally dominant tech product that has ever existed.
sentences.zh: 因此，至少在我看来，OpenAI 的野心首先是从谷歌手中夺取市场份额，谷歌是世界历史上最赚钱的企业之一，然后进攻 iPhone，这是有史以来最著名、在文化上占主导地位的科技产品。

blocks.html_en: I don’t know how it can do either of those things. It doesn’t seem like consumers even want these products or want AI at this scale. I don’t know where that revenue comes from in consumer, and the college students are all booing AI at graduation. Does OpenAI just need to pivot to enterprise? Does it need to spend its time spinning its wheels at consumer hardware devices before it figures out its business?

sentences.en: I don’t know how it can do either of those things.
sentences.zh: 我不知道它是如何做到这两件事的。

sentences.en: It doesn’t seem like consumers even want these products or want AI at this scale.
sentences.zh: 消费者似乎并不想要这些产品或想要这种规模的人工智能。

sentences.en: I don’t know where that revenue comes from in consumer, and the college students are all booing AI at graduation.
sentences.zh: 我不知道消费者的收入从哪里来，大学生毕业时都在嘘人工智能。

sentences.en: Does OpenAI just need to pivot to enterprise?
sentences.zh: OpenAI 是否只需要转向企业？

sentences.en: Does it need to spend its time spinning its wheels at consumer hardware devices before it figures out its business?
sentences.zh: 在弄清楚自己的业务之前，它是否需要花时间研究消费类硬件设备？

blocks.html_en: OpenAI has pivoted so many times internally. Its most recent pivot was that it was going to focus on its key revenue drivers, which are enterprise and coding. They’ve said that a million times. OpenAI folded Sora, Atlas, the sexting feature. It feels like years ago, but it’s crazy that that was in the past year.

sentences.en: OpenAI has pivoted so many times internally.
sentences.zh: OpenAI 在内部已经发生过多次转变。

sentences.en: Its most recent pivot was that it was going to focus on its key revenue drivers, which are enterprise and coding.
sentences.zh: 它最近的重点是将重点放在其主要收入驱动因素上，即企业和编码。

sentences.en: They’ve said that a million times.
sentences.zh: 他们已经说过一百万遍了。

sentences.en: OpenAI folded Sora, Atlas, the sexting feature.
sentences.zh: OpenAI 折叠了 Sora、Atlas、色情短信功能。

sentences.en: It feels like years ago, but it’s crazy that that was in the past year.
sentences.zh: 感觉就像是几年前的事了，但令人疯狂的是，那是在过去的一年里。

blocks.html_en: They’ve done a lot of this, and so, everyone thought, “Okay, they’re going for it, they’re doing enterprise,” because that’s the tried-and-true money maker here, especially in a year that they’re going to IPO. To be fair, yes, they had already planned to do the hardware device before this whole pivot and cutting out the side quests. I guess they can’t afford to cut out this potential side quest because they’ve already spent a lot on it, it’s happening no matter what. So now they’re just trying to make it as successful as it can.

sentences.en: They’ve done a lot of this, and so, everyone thought, “Okay, they’re going for it, they’re doing enterprise,” because that’s the tried-and-true money maker here, especially in a year that they’re going to IPO.
sentences.zh: 他们已经做了很多这样的事情，所以每个人都想，“好吧，他们正在努力，他们正在做企业”，因为这是这里经过考验的真正的赚钱工具，尤其是在他们即将进行 IPO 的一年。

sentences.en: To be fair, yes, they had already planned to do the hardware device before this whole pivot and cutting out the side quests.
sentences.zh: 公平地说，是的，他们已经计划在整个转向之前制作硬件设备并删除支线任务。

sentences.en: I guess they can’t afford to cut out this potential side quest because they’ve already spent a lot on it, it’s happening no matter what.
sentences.zh: 我想他们不能放弃这个潜在的支线任务，因为他们已经在上面花了很多钱，无论如何它都会发生。

sentences.en: So now they’re just trying to make it as successful as it can.
sentences.zh: 所以现在他们只是想尽其所能地取得成功。

blocks.html_en: I’ve only been on the AI beat for six years. That is a long time in AI, and maybe I’m missing something, but I just do not see a market when everyone is buying an OpenAI hardware device. If I’m wrong, we can come back on here and laugh at my clip from July 2026. A smart speaker, okay, maybe people buy it just to be a smart speaker like Google Home, that’s what I use mine for.

sentences.en: I’ve only been on the AI beat for six years.
sentences.zh: 我参与人工智能领域才六年。

sentences.en: That is a long time in AI, and maybe I’m missing something, but I just do not see a market when everyone is buying an OpenAI hardware device.
sentences.zh: 在人工智能领域已经有很长一段时间了，也许我错过了一些东西，但当每个人都在购买 OpenAI 硬件设备时，我只是看不到市场。

sentences.en: If I’m wrong, we can come back on here and laugh at my clip from July 2026.
sentences.zh: 如果我错了，我们可以回到这里并嘲笑我 2026 年 7 月的剪辑。

sentences.en: A smart speaker, okay, maybe people buy it just to be a smart speaker like Google Home, that’s what I use mine for.
sentences.zh: 智能音箱，好吧，也许人们买它只是为了成为像 Google Home 这样的智能音箱，这就是我用我的音箱的目的。

blocks.html_en: I think it’s going to be one of those things that a few people really, really, really love and that the general public probably does not adopt because it’s difficult to get them to adopt anything unless all their friends have it. There’s a social aspect. You’re going to be a laughingstock with green text if you don’t have an iPhone. No hate to Android people, I’m just saying sometimes you don’t get added to the group text or something. It’s sad and it’s not right, but there’s got to be some public buy-in if you’re going to be adopting a new piece of hardware a lot of the time.

sentences.en: I think it’s going to be one of those things that a few people really, really, really love and that the general public probably does not adopt because it’s difficult to get them to adopt anything unless all their friends have it.
sentences.zh: 我认为这将是少数人真正非常喜欢的东西之一，而公众可能不会采用，因为除非他们所有的朋友都拥有它，否则很难让他们采用任何东西。

sentences.en: There’s a social aspect.
sentences.zh: 有一个社会方面。

sentences.en: You’re going to be a laughingstock with green text if you don’t have an iPhone.
sentences.zh: 如果你没有 iPhone，你就会成为绿色文本的笑柄。

sentences.en: No hate to Android people, I’m just saying sometimes you don’t get added to the group text or something.
sentences.zh: 没有讨厌 Android 用户，我只是说有时你不会被添加到群组文本或其他内容中。

sentences.en: It’s sad and it’s not right, but there’s got to be some public buy-in if you’re going to be adopting a new piece of hardware a lot of the time.
sentences.zh: 这是可悲的，也是不对的，但如果你要经常采用新的硬件，就必须得到一些公众的支持。

blocks.html_en: We founded The Verge at the start of the smartphone boom. Just thinking back on it, it was never a question of whether people would adopt smartphones. That was obvious. It was obvious to us, maybe it wasn’t obvious to everyone, but we were in it and the only thing that wasn’t obvious was who would win.

sentences.en: We founded The Verge at the start of the smartphone boom.
sentences.zh: 我们在智能手机热潮开始时创立了 The Verge。

sentences.en: Just thinking back on it, it was never a question of whether people would adopt smartphones.
sentences.zh: 回想一下，人们是否会采用智能手机从来都不是问题。

sentences.en: That was obvious.
sentences.zh: 这是显而易见的。

sentences.en: It was obvious to us, maybe it wasn’t obvious to everyone, but we were in it and the only thing that wasn’t obvious was who would win.
sentences.zh: 这对我们来说是显而易见的，也许对每个人来说并不明显，但我们身在其中，唯一不明显的是谁会赢。

blocks.html_en: After a while it became obvious that Apple would win. How many iPhone killers did we cover 15 years ago? And they all came and went, and they looked like new operating systems. Windows Phone existed. The Palm Pre ran an operating system called webOS. Android was ascendant. Nokia tried to do its own thing and then they pivoted to Windows Phone.

sentences.en: After a while it became obvious that Apple would win.
sentences.zh: 过了一段时间，苹果获胜的局面就变得显而易见了。

sentences.en: How many iPhone killers did we cover 15 years ago?
sentences.zh: 15 年前我们报道了多少 iPhone 杀手？

sentences.en: And they all came and went, and they looked like new operating systems.
sentences.zh: 它们来来去去，看起来就像新的操作系统。

sentences.en: Windows Phone existed.
sentences.zh: Windows Phone 已经存在。

sentences.en: The Palm Pre ran an operating system called webOS.
sentences.zh: Palm Pre 运行一个名为 webOS 的操作系统。

sentences.en: Android was ascendant.
sentences.zh: Android 方兴未艾。

sentences.en: Nokia tried to do its own thing and then they pivoted to Windows Phone.
sentences.zh: 诺基亚尝试做自己的事情，然后转向 Windows Phone。

blocks.html_en: At the end of the day, we ended up with iOS and Android and everything else fell into line. Maybe the whole tech industry hates this and they hate paying the App Store fees, but this feels like that same kind of moment where OpenAI is going to put out a hardware device, it’s going to run its own operating system. Elon Musk is fulminating about Starlink and xAI putting out their own phone, maybe running their own operating system, apparently the run-up to IPO, they were showing it to people.

sentences.en: At the end of the day, we ended up with iOS and Android and everything else fell into line.
sentences.zh: 最终，我们最终选择了 iOS 和 Android，其他一切都顺理成章。

sentences.en: Maybe the whole tech industry hates this and they hate paying the App Store fees, but this feels like that same kind of moment where OpenAI is going to put out a hardware device, it’s going to run its own operating system.
sentences.zh: 也许整个科技行业都讨厌这一点，他们讨厌支付 App Store 费用，但这感觉就像 OpenAI 将推出硬件设备一样，它将运行自己的操作系统。

sentences.en: Elon Musk is fulminating about Starlink and xAI putting out their own phone, maybe running their own operating system, apparently the run-up to IPO, they were showing it to people.
sentences.zh: 埃隆·马斯克（Elon Musk）对 Starlink 和 xAI 推出自己的手机进行了猛烈抨击，可能运行自己的操作系统，显然是在 IPO 之前，他们向人们展示了它。

blocks.html_en: I just have really dumb questions that, to me, mirror the same questions we asked about new smartphone operating systems 15 years ago. Are you going to make a Gmail client? Is it going to have Instagram? Because if you don’t have those things, you don’t actually get to replace the phone. And if you don’t replace the phone, well, I’ve got the beta of iOS 27 on my iPhone right here and Siri AI is definitely good enough to just get through the day.

sentences.en: I just have really dumb questions that, to me, mirror the same questions we asked about new smartphone operating systems 15 years ago.
sentences.zh: 我只是有一些非常愚蠢的问题，对我来说，这些问题与 15 年前我们就新智能手机操作系统提出的问题一模一样。

sentences.en: Are you going to make a Gmail client?
sentences.zh: 你打算制作一个 Gmail 客户端吗？

sentences.en: Is it going to have Instagram?
sentences.zh: 会有 Instagram 吗？

sentences.en: Because if you don’t have those things, you don’t actually get to replace the phone.
sentences.zh: 因为如果你没有这些东西，你实际上就无法更换手机。

sentences.en: And if you don’t replace the phone, well, I’ve got the beta of iOS 27 on my iPhone right here and Siri AI is definitely good enough to just get through the day.
sentences.zh: 如果你不更换手机，那么，我的 iPhone 上已经安装了 iOS 27 测试版，Siri AI 绝对足以让你度过这一天。

blocks.html_en: That’s what really gets me here, because the smartphone replaced the dumb phone. So okay, you’ve got to have something way better than the smartphone. Are you going to? Doubtful, but we’ll see.

sentences.en: That’s what really gets me here, because the smartphone replaced the dumb phone.
sentences.zh: 这才是真正吸引我的地方，因为智能手机取代了普通手机。

sentences.en: So okay, you’ve got to have something way better than the smartphone.
sentences.zh: 所以好吧，你必须拥有比智能手机更好的东西。

sentences.en: Are you going to?
sentences.zh: 你要去吗？

sentences.en: Doubtful, but we’ll see.
sentences.zh: 值得怀疑，但我们会看到。

blocks.html_en: But the other thing that gets me here is how many devices OpenAI is reportedly working on. Reportedly it’s five hardware devices, and the screenless smart speaker is the first one. How can they come up with five? That’s what I want to know. What are the five? And shouldn’t you be putting all your resources toward one? It’s already hard to make one. I’m a little dubious of five successful AI hardware devices. Even one is a long shot, but five is a little bit crazy.

sentences.en: But the other thing that gets me here is how many devices OpenAI is reportedly working on.
sentences.zh: 但让我感兴趣的另一件事是据报道 OpenAI 正在支持多少设备。

sentences.en: Reportedly it’s five hardware devices, and the screenless smart speaker is the first one.
sentences.zh: 据了解，共有五款硬件设备，无屏智能音箱是第一款。

sentences.en: How can they come up with five?
sentences.zh: 他们怎么能想出五个呢？

sentences.en: That’s what I want to know.
sentences.zh: 这就是我想知道的。

sentences.en: What are the five?
sentences.zh: 这五个是什么？

sentences.en: And shouldn’t you be putting all your resources toward one?
sentences.zh: 难道你不应该把所有的资源都投入其中吗？

sentences.en: It’s already hard to make one.
sentences.zh: 想要制作一个已经很难了。

sentences.en: I’m a little dubious of five successful AI hardware devices.
sentences.zh: 我对五个成功的人工智能硬件设备有点怀疑。

sentences.en: Even one is a long shot, but five is a little bit crazy.
sentences.zh: 即使是一个也不太可能，但五个就有点疯狂了。

blocks.html_en: I’m sure there’s a lot and some will come and go. The thing that I think about, particularly when it comes to Jony Ive and his approach to hardware — which is familiar at this point, we’ve watched him launch a lot of hardware at Apple — is Ive tends to focus on new input methods for new devices.

sentences.en: I’m sure there’s a lot and some will come and go.
sentences.zh: 我确信有很多，有些会来来去去。

sentences.en: The thing that I think about, particularly when it comes to Jony Ive and his approach to hardware — which is familiar at this point, we’ve watched him launch a lot of hardware at Apple — is Ive tends to focus on new input methods for new devices.
sentences.zh: 我想到的一件事，特别是当谈到 Jony Ive 和他的硬件方法时——这一点很熟悉，我们已经看到他在苹果推出了很多硬件——Ive 倾向于专注于新设备的新输入方法。

blocks.html_en: The iPod had the click wheel and that really defined the form and function of the iPod. The iPhone has a multitouch screen that really defined the form and function of the iPhone and then the iPad. If you’ll remember the Apple Watch, they launched it with great fanfare about the scroll wheel on the side. I don’t know if that came to pass, but they really thought that was a big deal when they launched the Apple Watch.

sentences.en: The iPod had the click wheel and that really defined the form and function of the iPod.
sentences.zh: iPod 具有点击轮，这真正定义了 iPod 的形式和功能。

sentences.en: The iPhone has a multitouch screen that really defined the form and function of the iPhone and then the iPad.
sentences.zh: iPhone 拥有多点触控屏幕，它真正定义了 iPhone 和 iPad 的外形和功能。

sentences.en: If you’ll remember the Apple Watch, they launched it with great fanfare about the scroll wheel on the side.
sentences.zh: 如果你还记得 Apple Watch，他们在推出这款手表时就大肆宣扬了侧面的滚轮。

sentences.en: I don’t know if that came to pass, but they really thought that was a big deal when they launched the Apple Watch.
sentences.zh: 我不知道这是否成为现实，但当他们推出 Apple Watch 时，他们确实认为这是一件大事。

blocks.html_en: This is Ive’s go-to path: “We find a new input mechanism, we’re going to define the product around it.” I know exactly what that should be for AI — it’s a natural language input. You can just tell the computer whatever you want and it’ll figure it out and it’ll go off and do it for you.

sentences.en: This is Ive’s go-to path: “We find a new input mechanism, we’re going to define the product around it.”
sentences.zh: 这是艾维的首选路径：“我们找到了一种新的输入机制，我们将围绕它定义产品。”

sentences.en: I know exactly what that should be for AI — it’s a natural language input.
sentences.zh: 我确切地知道人工智能应该是什么——它是一种自然语言输入。

sentences.en: You can just tell the computer whatever you want and it’ll figure it out and it’ll go off and do it for you.
sentences.zh: 你只需告诉计算机你想要什么，它就会弄清楚并为你做。

blocks.html_en: But we’re however many years into the AI revolution?Three or four years into this version of it? And it doesn’t quite work yet. I think this is why the AI devices keep failing, as you keep mentioning. Because you can talk to a Humane Pin all day and all night and it will pretend to understand you and then not actually be able to do the things on the other side of it.

sentences.en: But we’re however many years into the AI revolution?Three or four years into this version of it?
sentences.zh: 但我们已经进入人工智能革命多少年了？这个版本已经三四年了？

sentences.en: And it doesn’t quite work yet.
sentences.zh: 而且它还没有完全发挥作用。

sentences.en: I think this is why the AI devices keep failing, as you keep mentioning.
sentences.zh: 正如你一直提到的，我认为这就是人工智能设备不断失败的原因。

sentences.en: Because you can talk to a Humane Pin all day and all night and it will pretend to understand you and then not actually be able to do the things on the other side of it.
sentences.zh: 因为你可以整天整夜地与人道别针交谈，它会假装理解你，但实际上却无法做它另一边的事情。

blocks.html_en: That feels like the problem in AI right now. There’s these flashes of brilliance and then there’s these limits of capability. Where do you think we are in that process of figuring out exactly what it can and can’t do? And how do you think that limits a hardware device?

sentences.en: That feels like the problem in AI right now.
sentences.zh: 这感觉就像人工智能现在的问题。

sentences.en: There’s these flashes of brilliance and then there’s these limits of capability.
sentences.zh: 有这些闪光，然后有这些能力的限制。

sentences.en: Where do you think we are in that process of figuring out exactly what it can and can’t do?
sentences.zh: 您认为我们在弄清楚它能做什么和不能做什么的过程中处于什么阶段？

sentences.en: And how do you think that limits a hardware device?
sentences.zh: 您认为这如何限制硬件设备？

blocks.html_en: We’re still in the midst of it. I personally use AI all the time, obviously in my personal life to test things out, see what it can do. Month to month, I see how it’s improved, at least from my own personal experience, which is all anecdotal.

sentences.en: We’re still in the midst of it.
sentences.zh: 我们仍处于其中。

sentences.en: I personally use AI all the time, obviously in my personal life to test things out, see what it can do.
sentences.zh: 我个人一直在使用人工智能，显然是在我的个人生活中进行测试，看看它能做什么。

sentences.en: Month to month, I see how it’s improved, at least from my own personal experience, which is all anecdotal.
sentences.zh: 每个月，我都会看到它是如何改进的，至少从我自己的个人经验来看，这都是轶事。

blocks.html_en: But just the other day I was trying to look up a lot of addresses of companies because I wanted to map something out. I would say maybe 60 percent of the ones I looked up were completely wrong, just wrong. It made me a whole table. And it was like, “Yeah, don’t worry, all these are definitely correct.” And then 60 percent wrong. I’m like, “A lot of these are wrong.” They’re like, “Oh, sorry, my mistake.”

sentences.en: But just the other day I was trying to look up a lot of addresses of companies because I wanted to map something out.
sentences.zh: 但就在前几天，我试图查找很多公司的地址，因为我想找出一些东西。

sentences.en: I would say maybe 60 percent of the ones I looked up were completely wrong, just wrong.
sentences.zh: 我想说也许我查到的 60% 都是完全错误的，只是错误的。

sentences.en: It made me a whole table.
sentences.zh: 它让我占了一整桌。

sentences.en: And it was like, “Yeah, don’t worry, all these are definitely correct.”
sentences.zh: 就像是，“是的，别担心，所有这些绝对是正确的。”

sentences.en: And then 60 percent wrong.
sentences.zh: 然后百分之六十错了。

sentences.en: I’m like, “A lot of these are wrong.”
sentences.zh: 我想，“其中很多都是错误的。”

sentences.en: They’re like, “Oh, sorry, my mistake.”
sentences.zh: 他们就像，“哦，对不起，我的错。”

blocks.html_en: Yeah, my favorite.

sentences.en: Yeah, my favorite.
sentences.zh: 是的，我最喜欢的。

blocks.html_en: It’s like, “Oh yeah, you’re right. You caught me.”

sentences.en: It’s like, “Oh yeah, you’re right.
sentences.zh: 就像，“哦，是的，你是对的。

sentences.en: You caught me.”
sentences.zh: 你抓住了我。”

blocks.html_en: It’s right about a lot of stuff a lot of the time. But as we all know from when we were an intern back in the day, or if we’ve ever had an intern, there are certain things that are just easier to do yourself if you’re not 100 percent sure the person is going to get it right.

sentences.en: It’s right about a lot of stuff a lot of the time.
sentences.zh: 很多时候，很多事情都是正确的。

sentences.en: But as we all know from when we were an intern back in the day, or if we’ve ever had an intern, there are certain things that are just easier to do yourself if you’re not 100 percent sure the person is going to get it right.
sentences.zh: 但我们都知道，从我们以前还是实习生的时候，或者如果我们曾经有过实习生，如果你不能 100% 确定这个人会做对，有些事情你自己做会更容易。

blocks.html_en: So what I wonder is, for a hardware device like this, there are a lot of low-stakes questions you can ask it and it’ll be fine. But for higher-stakes things, it’s simpler to just look it up yourself or do it yourself even than put it out there when it’s going to be a potentially wrong answer.

sentences.en: So what I wonder is, for a hardware device like this, there are a lot of low-stakes questions you can ask it and it’ll be fine.
sentences.zh: 所以我想知道的是，对于像这样的硬件设备，有很多低风险的问题你可以问它，那就没问题了。

sentences.en: But for higher-stakes things, it’s simpler to just look it up yourself or do it yourself even than put it out there when it’s going to be a potentially wrong answer.
sentences.zh: 但对于风险更高的事情，自己查找或自己做比在可能是错误答案时将其放在那里更简单。

blocks.html_en: Is this going to end up like a Google Home situation, where you’re just using it as a speaker and you’re asking it what the weather is because it doesn’t really matter that much if it’s wrong? But as we know, it can only really be super useful if you can trust it with those higher-stakes types of questions. And so, saying, “Hey, what time does Emily’s flight get in tonight because she’s staying with me? What flight is she on?” Or, “Is it delayed?” If it gets that wrong, okay, I’m not going to be ready for her to come in from the airport.

sentences.en: Is this going to end up like a Google Home situation, where you’re just using it as a speaker and you’re asking it what the weather is because it doesn’t really matter that much if it’s wrong?
sentences.zh: 这是否会像 Google Home 的情况一样，你只是将它用作扬声器，然后询问它天气如何，因为如果它出错了也没有那么重要？

sentences.en: But as we know, it can only really be super useful if you can trust it with those higher-stakes types of questions.
sentences.zh: 但正如我们所知，只有当您能够信任它来解决那些高风险类型的问题时，它才会真正非常有用。

sentences.en: And so, saying, “Hey, what time does Emily’s flight get in tonight because she’s staying with me?
sentences.zh: 所以，说：“嘿，艾米丽的航班今晚几点起飞，因为她和我住在一起？

sentences.en: What flight is she on?”
sentences.zh: 她坐哪趟航班？”

sentences.en: Or, “Is it delayed?”
sentences.zh: 或者，“有延迟吗？”

sentences.en: If it gets that wrong, okay, I’m not going to be ready for her to come in from the airport.
sentences.zh: 如果出了问题，好吧，我不会准备好让她从机场回来。

blocks.html_en: This does seem like the core problem. Will there be enough progress? Certainly the industry will claim that it’s made great progress on hallucinations. Whether that’s the lived experience of people who use these products, I think that’s up for debate. But the industry claims they’ve made great progress here.

sentences.en: This does seem like the core problem.
sentences.zh: 这看起来确实是核心问题。

sentences.en: Will there be enough progress?
sentences.zh: 会有足够的进展吗？

sentences.en: Certainly the industry will claim that it’s made great progress on hallucinations.
sentences.zh: 当然，该行业会声称在幻觉方面取得了巨大进步。

sentences.en: Whether that’s the lived experience of people who use these products, I think that’s up for debate.
sentences.zh: 这是否是使用这些产品的人的生活体验，我认为这是有争议的。

sentences.en: But the industry claims they’ve made great progress here.
sentences.zh: 但业界声称他们在这方面取得了巨大进展。

blocks.html_en: The other piece is, will OpenAI have access to all of your data and personal context to make this useful in an environment with a smart speaker, where you can just say whatever you want to it? That, again, brings you back to whether it’s going to have a Gmail client. Where’s the Gmail client going to run? Are you going to let OpenAI log into your Gmail, so when you ask for flight information, it can go look at it? Or are you just going to default to opening Gmail on your phone and asking it because Google already has it? This seems like a version of competition that really depends on how comfortable you are handing over all of your data to various AI providers. And I’m just not sure OpenAI is that one.

sentences.en: The other piece is, will OpenAI have access to all of your data and personal context to make this useful in an environment with a smart speaker, where you can just say whatever you want to it?
sentences.zh: 另一个问题是，OpenAI 是否可以访问您的所有数据和个人背景，使其在带有智能扬声器的环境中发挥作用，让您可以随心所欲地说出任何话？

sentences.en: That, again, brings you back to whether it’s going to have a Gmail client.
sentences.zh: 这又让你回想起它是否会有 Gmail 客户端。

sentences.en: Where’s the Gmail client going to run?
sentences.zh: Gmail 客户端将在哪里运行？

sentences.en: Are you going to let OpenAI log into your Gmail, so when you ask for flight information, it can go look at it?
sentences.zh: 你打算让 OpenAI 登录你的 Gmail，这样当你询问航班信息时，它就可以去看吗？

sentences.en: Or are you just going to default to opening Gmail on your phone and asking it because Google already has it?
sentences.zh: 或者你只是默认在手机上打开 Gmail 并询问它，因为 Google 已经有了它？

sentences.en: This seems like a version of competition that really depends on how comfortable you are handing over all of your data to various AI providers.
sentences.zh: 这似乎是一种竞争，实际上取决于您将所有数据移交给各个人工智能提供商的舒适程度。

sentences.en: And I’m just not sure OpenAI is that one.
sentences.zh: 我只是不确定 OpenAI 就是那个。

blocks.html_en: The other part of this is that — and obviously this is a vocal minority here, it’s not like the majority of OpenAI users — a lot of people are extremely worried about ads. Anthropic capitalized on that with its Super Bowl ads that were anti-AI ads. OpenAI has been dealing with that fallout and just trying to say, “Hey, don’t worry, we’re not going to influence answers, we’re just going to raise a similar ad underneath the answer.”

sentences.en: The other part of this is that — and obviously this is a vocal minority here, it’s not like the majority of OpenAI users — a lot of people are extremely worried about ads.
sentences.zh: 另一方面是——显然这是少数敢于发声的人，与大多数 OpenAI 用户不同——很多人都非常担心广告。

sentences.en: Anthropic capitalized on that with its Super Bowl ads that were anti-AI ads.
sentences.zh: Anthropic 利用这一点推出了反人工智能广告的超级碗广告。

sentences.en: OpenAI has been dealing with that fallout and just trying to say, “Hey, don’t worry, we’re not going to influence answers, we’re just going to raise a similar ad underneath the answer.”
sentences.zh: OpenAI 一直在应对这一后果，只是想说：“嘿，别担心，我们不会影响答案，我们只是在答案下方发布类似的广告。”

blocks.html_en: But it can be unnerving, whenever I use ChatGPT and an ad surfaces. It just makes me feel like it’s less private. It’s just a little bit strange and I feel like everyone gets that unnerved feeling. Even if it’s not affecting the actual answer, it’s still reminding you that it’s not a space where they’re not trying to sell you something. Someone is always trying to sell you something.

sentences.en: But it can be unnerving, whenever I use ChatGPT and an ad surfaces.
sentences.zh: 但每当我使用 ChatGPT 并出现广告时，这可能会令人不安。

sentences.en: It just makes me feel like it’s less private.
sentences.zh: 这只是让我觉得它不太私密。

sentences.en: It’s just a little bit strange and I feel like everyone gets that unnerved feeling.
sentences.zh: 这有点奇怪，我觉得每个人都会有那种不安的感觉。

sentences.en: Even if it’s not affecting the actual answer, it’s still reminding you that it’s not a space where they’re not trying to sell you something.
sentences.zh: 即使它不影响实际答案，它仍然提醒你，这不是一个他们不想向你推销东西的空间。

sentences.en: Someone is always trying to sell you something.
sentences.zh: 总是有人想卖给你东西。

blocks.html_en: There’s already a lot of loss of trust right now in AI labs, if anyone trusted them to begin with. Right now, it’s at an all-time low. We’re seeing the rise of AI populism. We’re seeing lots of death threats against AI CEOs, as The Wall Street Journal covered the other day. There’s a lot of backlash, like you were saying about college kids booing AI at graduations. We’re seeing a huge backlash right now, and I just think it’s a strange time to be releasing a public-facing, “every consumer should buy this” AI hardware device.

sentences.en: There’s already a lot of loss of trust right now in AI labs, if anyone trusted them to begin with.
sentences.zh: 如果有人一开始就信任人工智能实验室的话，现在已经失去了很多信任。

sentences.en: Right now, it’s at an all-time low.
sentences.zh: 目前，它处于历史最低点。

sentences.en: We’re seeing the rise of AI populism.
sentences.zh: 我们看到人工智能民粹主义的兴起。

sentences.en: We’re seeing lots of death threats against AI CEOs, as The Wall Street Journal covered the other day.
sentences.zh: 正如《华尔街日报》前几天报道的那样，我们看到很多针对人工智能首席执行官的死亡威胁。

sentences.en: There’s a lot of backlash, like you were saying about college kids booing AI at graduations.
sentences.zh: 有很多强烈的反对，就像你所说的大学生在毕业典礼上嘘人工智能一样。

sentences.en: We’re seeing a huge backlash right now, and I just think it’s a strange time to be releasing a public-facing, “every consumer should buy this” AI hardware device.
sentences.zh: 我们现在看到了巨大的反弹，我只是认为现在发布面向公众的“每个消费者都应该购买这个”人工智能硬件设备是一个奇怪的时机。

blocks.html_en: That connects to a bigger issue for OpenAI in this Apple lawsuit, which is that the AI industry right now is basically built on unauthorized taking. I’m saying that instead of theft because there are a lot of lawsuits. I know our audience wants me to just call it theft and maybe it is just theft. And maybe we can just say that when we get to the end of the road of 10,000 different lawsuits. But that part of the AI industry feels like we have to studiously ignore it.

sentences.en: That connects to a bigger issue for OpenAI in this Apple lawsuit, which is that the AI industry right now is basically built on unauthorized taking.
sentences.zh: 这与 OpenAI 在苹果诉讼中面临的一个更大问题有关，即目前的人工智能行业基本上是建立在未经授权的获取之上的。

sentences.en: I’m saying that instead of theft because there are a lot of lawsuits.
sentences.zh: 我说的是不是盗窃，因为有很多诉讼。

sentences.en: I know our audience wants me to just call it theft and maybe it is just theft.
sentences.zh: 我知道我们的观众希望我称之为盗窃，也许这只是盗窃。

sentences.en: And maybe we can just say that when we get to the end of the road of 10,000 different lawsuits.
sentences.zh: 也许当我们走到 10,000 起不同诉讼之路的尽头时，我们就可以这么说。

sentences.en: But that part of the AI industry feels like we have to studiously ignore it.
sentences.zh: 但人工智能行业的这一部分感觉我们必须刻意忽视它。

blocks.html_en: There’s a trillion-dollar question over whether training data is fair use that is as of yet unresolved. We can just go down the list of lawsuits where all of these companies have to defend themselves about training data. And if they lose any one of them, maybe this whole industry cost structure falls apart.

sentences.en: There’s a trillion-dollar question over whether training data is fair use that is as of yet unresolved.
sentences.zh: 关于训练数据是否合理使用，还有一个价值数万亿美元的问题尚未解决。

sentences.en: We can just go down the list of lawsuits where all of these companies have to defend themselves about training data.
sentences.zh: 我们可以列出所有这些公司都必须就训练数据为自己辩护的诉讼清单。

sentences.en: And if they lose any one of them, maybe this whole industry cost structure falls apart.
sentences.zh: 如果他们失去其中任何一个，也许整个行业的成本结构就会崩溃。

blocks.html_en: The inputs to AI right now are free. They just took the internet and they took all the books. We just saw, as a result of a hack, that Suno had to basically admit that it had scraped all of the music off of YouTube illegally. It just didn’t have permission, and I think YouTube feels a certain way about that and certainly the artists on YouTube feel a certain way about that.

sentences.en: The inputs to AI right now are free.
sentences.zh: 目前人工智能的输入是免费的。

sentences.en: They just took the internet and they took all the books.
sentences.zh: 他们拿走了互联网，拿走了所有的书。

sentences.en: We just saw, as a result of a hack, that Suno had to basically admit that it had scraped all of the music off of YouTube illegally.
sentences.zh: 我们刚刚看到，由于黑客攻击，Suno 基本上不得不承认它非法从 YouTube 上删除了所有音乐。

sentences.en: It just didn’t have permission, and I think YouTube feels a certain way about that and certainly the artists on YouTube feel a certain way about that.
sentences.zh: 它只是没有获得许可，我认为 YouTube 对此有一定的看法，当然 YouTube 上的艺术家也有一定的看法。

blocks.html_en: Here’s Apple saying OpenAI also took its hardware designs and its manufacturing know-how. It didn’t have permission for that either, those are Apple’s secrets. OpenAI hired people and they concocted this scheme to take all of that stuff without permission.

sentences.en: Here’s Apple saying OpenAI also took its hardware designs and its manufacturing know-how.
sentences.zh: Apple 表示 OpenAI 还采用了其硬件设计和制造技术。

sentences.en: It didn’t have permission for that either, those are Apple’s secrets.
sentences.zh: 它也没有获得许可，这些是苹果的秘密。

sentences.en: OpenAI hired people and they concocted this scheme to take all of that stuff without permission.
sentences.zh: OpenAI 雇佣了一些人，他们策划了这个计划，在未经许可的情况下拿走所有这些东西。

blocks.html_en: Again, culturally, this just feels like part of the AI industry to me, that they think they can just take everything to build super intelligence as fast as they can. And once they get there, all will be forgiven. But the public doesn’t seem to like it. Certainly, artists and writers and musicians don’t seem to like it, and now Apple doesn’t like it.

sentences.en: Again, culturally, this just feels like part of the AI industry to me, that they think they can just take everything to build super intelligence as fast as they can.
sentences.zh: 再说一次，从文化上来说，这对我来说就像是人工智能行业的一部分，他们认为他们可以尽一切努力尽快构建超级智能。

sentences.en: And once they get there, all will be forgiven.
sentences.zh: 一旦他们到达那里，一切都会被原谅。

sentences.en: But the public doesn’t seem to like it.
sentences.zh: 但公众似乎并不喜欢它。

sentences.en: Certainly, artists and writers and musicians don’t seem to like it, and now Apple doesn’t like it.
sentences.zh: 当然，艺术家、作家和音乐家似乎不喜欢它，现在苹果也不喜欢它。

blocks.html_en: It feels like this lawsuit is going to be a proxy for that as much as anything else. Can you take all this stuff without permission? I’m not sure OpenAI has the cultural capital to fight back. What are they going to say?

sentences.en: It feels like this lawsuit is going to be a proxy for that as much as anything else.
sentences.zh: 感觉这起诉讼将像其他任何事情一样成为这一点的代表。

sentences.en: Can you take all this stuff without permission?
sentences.zh: 未经许可你能拿走所有这些东西吗？

sentences.en: I’m not sure OpenAI has the cultural capital to fight back.
sentences.zh: 我不确定 OpenAI 是否有文化资本进行反击。

sentences.en: What are they going to say?
sentences.zh: 他们会说什么？

blocks.html_en: It’s really ironic that this whole industry is only possible because of the amount of data it’s consumed, whether aboveboard or not. And AI labs, as well as just tech companies in general, get so litigious when others take that information elsewhere. So this whole thing is pretty ironic.

sentences.en: It’s really ironic that this whole industry is only possible because of the amount of data it’s consumed, whether aboveboard or not.
sentences.zh: 真正具有讽刺意味的是，整个行业之所以能够存在，只是因为它所消耗的数据量，无论是否光明正大。

sentences.en: And AI labs, as well as just tech companies in general, get so litigious when others take that information elsewhere.
sentences.zh: 当其他人将这些信息带到其他地方时，人工智能实验室以及一般的科技公司就会变得如此诉讼。

sentences.en: So this whole thing is pretty ironic.
sentences.zh: 所以这整件事非常讽刺。

blocks.html_en: The AI industry is really famously cliquish. It’s almost incestuous, it’s like you work with the same people here, then you depart and go elsewhere. Then that same company buys your start-up that you created and then you leave that start-up to go somewhere else and you get acqui-hired by your old friend that worked at this other place. A lot of industries are like that, but this industry specifically is so much like that. And so yeah, I think it’s a bit ironic on both counts.

sentences.en: The AI industry is really famously cliquish.
sentences.zh: 人工智能行业确实是出了名的小集团。

sentences.en: It’s almost incestuous, it’s like you work with the same people here, then you depart and go elsewhere.
sentences.zh: 这几乎是乱伦，就像你在这里和同样的人一起工作，然后你离开并去其他地方。

sentences.en: Then that same company buys your start-up that you created and then you leave that start-up to go somewhere else and you get acqui-hired by your old friend that worked at this other place.
sentences.zh: 然后同一家公司收购了你创建的初创公司，然后你离开该初创公司去其他地方，你被在另一个地方工作的老朋友收购了。

sentences.en: A lot of industries are like that, but this industry specifically is so much like that.
sentences.zh: 很多行业都是这样，但这个行业尤其如此。

sentences.en: And so yeah, I think it’s a bit ironic on both counts.
sentences.zh: 是的，我认为这在这两方面都有点讽刺。

blocks.html_en: People are going to leave Apple, and okay, they’re going to take some trade secrets. Was this incredibly egregious? Yes, some of these claims are crazy. But also, like you were mentioning, a lot of the reason that any of these companies are even successful in the first place is because of all the unauthorized taking they’ve done. It’s an interesting time to be watching all this play out.

sentences.en: People are going to leave Apple, and okay, they’re going to take some trade secrets.
sentences.zh: 人们将会离开苹果，好吧，他们会带走一些商业秘密。

sentences.en: Was this incredibly egregious?
sentences.zh: 这是否令人难以置信？

sentences.en: Yes, some of these claims are crazy.
sentences.zh: 是的，其中一些说法很疯狂。

sentences.en: But also, like you were mentioning, a lot of the reason that any of these companies are even successful in the first place is because of all the unauthorized taking they’ve done.
sentences.zh: 而且，正如您所提到的，这些公司之所以能够成功，很大程度上是因为他们所做的所有未经授权的获取。

sentences.en: It’s an interesting time to be watching all this play out.
sentences.zh: 这是一个观看这一切上演的有趣时刻。

blocks.html_en: All the AI companies get really mad when, I don’t know, Chinese AI companies distill their models. I’m just thinking about a jury and how Apple will play this case to a jury and be like, “You stole all the music, didn’t you? And then you also stole our manufacturing designs. And don’t you get mad when the Chinese companies distill ChatGPT?”

sentences.en: All the AI companies get really mad when, I don’t know, Chinese AI companies distill their models.
sentences.zh: 我不知道，当中国人工智能公司提炼他们的模型时，所有人工智能公司都会非常生气。

sentences.en: I’m just thinking about a jury and how Apple will play this case to a jury and be like, “You stole all the music, didn’t you?
sentences.zh: 我只是在考虑陪审团，以及苹果将如何向陪审团审理此案，并说：“你偷了所有音乐，不是吗？

sentences.en: And then you also stole our manufacturing designs.
sentences.zh: 然后你还窃取了我们的制造设计。

sentences.en: And don’t you get mad when the Chinese companies distill ChatGPT?”
sentences.zh: 当中国公司提取 ChatGPT 时，你不会生气吗？”

blocks.html_en: In the course of your reporting, does it ever occur to the people in the AI industry that being mad about distillation is the exact same feeling that artists and musicians have about all of their work being put into the model in the first place?

sentences.en: In the course of your reporting, does it ever occur to the people in the AI industry that being mad about distillation is the exact same feeling that artists and musicians have about all of their work being put into the model in the first place?
sentences.zh: 在您的报道过程中，人工智能行业的人们是否曾经想过，对蒸馏的疯狂与艺术家和音乐家最初将他们的所有作品放入模型中的感受完全相同？

blocks.html_en: I haven’t seen any of them even acknowledge that or at least even hint toward it one time, which is crazy. It just feels like there’s a disconnect there. They get so, so angry about distillation, they’re saying, “It’s against our terms of service!” They can’t even say it’s illegal, just against the terms of service. They write open letters to the government. They even come together with their mortal enemies and other AI labs to unite against distillation. So, it’s just interesting to me thatI’ve never seen that occur to them.

sentences.en: I haven’t seen any of them even acknowledge that or at least even hint toward it one time, which is crazy.
sentences.zh: 我还没有看到他们中的任何一个人承认这一点，或者至少一次暗示过这一点，这太疯狂了。

sentences.en: It just feels like there’s a disconnect there.
sentences.zh: 只是感觉那里有一种脱节。

sentences.en: They get so, so angry about distillation, they’re saying, “It’s against our terms of service!”
sentences.zh: 他们对蒸馏非常非常愤怒，他们说：“这违反了我们的服务条款！”

sentences.en: They can’t even say it’s illegal, just against the terms of service.
sentences.zh: 他们甚至不能说这是非法的，只是违反了服务条款。

sentences.en: They write open letters to the government.
sentences.zh: 他们给政府写公开信。

sentences.en: They even come together with their mortal enemies and other AI labs to unite against distillation.
sentences.zh: 他们甚至与他们的死敌和其他人工智能实验室联合起来反对蒸馏。

sentences.en: So, it’s just interesting to me thatI’ve never seen that occur to them.
sentences.zh: 所以，对我来说有趣的是我从未见过他们发生过这种事。

blocks.html_en: I know a lot of people are probably listening to this and that they’re mad at me, and mad at us, for not making the claim that Apple is just filing this lawsuit because it was behind in AI. It missed with Apple Intelligence, it took a long time to catch back up, it’s only shipping it now. It had to go to Google and distill Google’s Gemini models and put them in its new Private Cloud Compute system, and all the stuff that it needed to do. But that is shipping now.

sentences.en: I know a lot of people are probably listening to this and that they’re mad at me, and mad at us, for not making the claim that Apple is just filing this lawsuit because it was behind in AI.
sentences.zh: 我知道很多人可能都在听这个，他们生我的气，生我们的气，因为他们没有声称苹果只是因为在人工智能方面落后而提起这场诉讼。

sentences.en: It missed with Apple Intelligence, it took a long time to catch back up, it’s only shipping it now.
sentences.zh: 它错过了Apple Intelligence，花了很长时间才追上，现在才发货。

sentences.en: It had to go to Google and distill Google’s Gemini models and put them in its new Private Cloud Compute system, and all the stuff that it needed to do.
sentences.zh: 它必须去谷歌，提炼谷歌的 Gemini 模型，并将它们放入新的私有云计算系统中，以及它需要做的所有事情。

sentences.en: But that is shipping now.
sentences.zh: 但现在正在发货。

blocks.html_en: Do you feel like the broader AI industry is just looking at Apple lashing out at being late? Because I have heard a lot of glimmers of that, that this is just Apple trying to slow down the biggest threat in consumer as it launches Apple Intelligence and tries to catch back up.

sentences.en: Do you feel like the broader AI industry is just looking at Apple lashing out at being late?
sentences.zh: 您是否觉得更广泛的人工智能行业正在关注苹果公司的迟到？

sentences.en: Because I have heard a lot of glimmers of that, that this is just Apple trying to slow down the biggest threat in consumer as it launches Apple Intelligence and tries to catch back up.
sentences.zh: 因为我听过很多这样的说法，这只是苹果试图减缓消费者面临的最大威胁，因为它推出了 Apple Intelligence，并试图迎头赶上。

blocks.html_en: That could be definitely part of it. Everyone knows Apple has been famously incredibly behind in AI and it’s been a big topic of conversation and an embarrassment for the company for years. So, that could definitely be part of it.

sentences.en: That could be definitely part of it.
sentences.zh: 这绝对是其中的一部分。

sentences.en: Everyone knows Apple has been famously incredibly behind in AI and it’s been a big topic of conversation and an embarrassment for the company for years.
sentences.zh: 大家都知道，苹果在人工智能方面的落后是出了名的，多年来，这一直是人们谈论的一个大话题，也是该公司的尴尬之处。

sentences.en: So, that could definitely be part of it.
sentences.zh: 所以，这肯定是其中的一部分。

blocks.html_en: But this lawsuit is not about someone “stealing trade secrets” for Siri. It’s about hardware, specifically “show and tell” of hardware, things like that. Hardware is something that Apple does amazingly and they are pretty much by all accounts the best at.

sentences.en: But this lawsuit is not about someone “stealing trade secrets” for Siri.
sentences.zh: 但这起诉讼并不是关于有人为 Siri“窃取商业机密”。

sentences.en: It’s about hardware, specifically “show and tell” of hardware, things like that.
sentences.zh: 这是关于硬件的，特别是“展示和讲述”硬件之类的事情。

sentences.en: Hardware is something that Apple does amazingly and they are pretty much by all accounts the best at.
sentences.zh: 苹果在硬件方面做得非常出色，而且从各方面来看，他们几乎都是最擅长的。

blocks.html_en: So, I think some people are thinking that. It’s a benefit for Apple if they’re able to kneecap a competitor from pulling ahead in any way. But the fact that this is a trade secrets lawsuit about hardware specifically I think has rolled back a few of those claims; it’s not like they’re trying to claim that OpenAI is stealing Siri AI’s secrets that it may not have many of.

sentences.en: So, I think some people are thinking that.
sentences.zh: 所以，我认为有些人正在这么想。

sentences.en: It’s a benefit for Apple if they’re able to kneecap a competitor from pulling ahead in any way.
sentences.zh: 如果苹果能够以任何方式阻止竞争对手取得领先，这对苹果来说是一件好事。

sentences.en: But the fact that this is a trade secrets lawsuit about hardware specifically I think has rolled back a few of those claims; it’s not like they’re trying to claim that OpenAI is stealing Siri AI’s secrets that it may not have many of.
sentences.zh: 但我认为，这是一起专门针对硬件的商业秘密诉讼，这一事实已经推翻了其中一些主张；他们并不是想声称 OpenAI 正在窃取 Siri AI 的秘密，而 Siri 可能并不拥有很多秘密。

blocks.html_en: [Laughs] They’re like, “Please take everything we know about Siri AI. That will truly kneecap you.” Apple can afford the distraction, you’ve mentioned this already in this conversation. Apple can just hire a bunch of lawyers and set them off to be as tenacious as they want. Maybe the rest of Apple won’t even notice that that’s happening, because they’re busy making the next iPhone.

sentences.en: [Laughs] They’re like, “Please take everything we know about Siri AI.
sentences.zh: [笑]他们就像是，“请利用我们所知道的关于 Siri AI 的一切。

sentences.en: That will truly kneecap you.”
sentences.zh: 那真的会让你跪倒在地。”

sentences.en: Apple can afford the distraction, you’ve mentioned this already in this conversation.
sentences.zh: 苹果可以承受这种干扰，你已经在这次谈话中提到了这一点。

sentences.en: Apple can just hire a bunch of lawyers and set them off to be as tenacious as they want.
sentences.zh: 苹果可以雇佣一群律师，让他们按照自己的意愿顽强地坚持下去。

sentences.en: Maybe the rest of Apple won’t even notice that that’s happening, because they’re busy making the next iPhone.
sentences.zh: 也许苹果的其他部门甚至不会注意到这种情况正在发生，因为他们正忙于制造下一代 iPhone。

blocks.html_en: Apple spent however many years and like $10 billion trying to make a car and it just didn’t work. It didn’t even occur to the rest of Apple that was happening. They just kept shipping phones and laptops and everything was just fine.

sentences.en: Apple spent however many years and like $10 billion trying to make a car and it just didn’t work.
sentences.zh: 不管苹果花了多少年时间和大约 100 亿美元试图制造一辆汽车，但都没有成功。

sentences.en: It didn’t even occur to the rest of Apple that was happening.
sentences.zh: 苹果的其他部门甚至没有想到正在发生这样的事情。

sentences.en: They just kept shipping phones and laptops and everything was just fine.
sentences.zh: 他们只是继续运送手机和笔记本电脑，一切都很好。

blocks.html_en: OpenAI is not that company. OpenAI might not even have enough executives to absorb the cost of another distraction. They keep leaving. Greg Brockman, who’s the president of OpenAI, seems to be running the entire company himself.

sentences.en: OpenAI is not that company.
sentences.zh: OpenAI 不是那家公司。

sentences.en: OpenAI might not even have enough executives to absorb the cost of another distraction.
sentences.zh: OpenAI 甚至可能没有足够的高管来承担另一次干扰的成本。

sentences.en: They keep leaving.
sentences.zh: 他们不断离开。

sentences.en: Greg Brockman, who’s the president of OpenAI, seems to be running the entire company himself.
sentences.zh: OpenAI 总裁格雷格·布罗克曼 (Greg Brockman) 似乎亲自运营着整个公司。

blocks.html_en: As you’ve been reporting on the story, has it occurred to OpenAI that they’re going to need more people to do more things? Because I look at that company and they just keep shedding all of their talent and pivoting, as you said, from one thing to the other and they’ve landed on “we need to compete with Anthropic.” But it doesn’t seem like they can afford distraction at this scale in any way, shape, or form.

sentences.en: As you’ve been reporting on the story, has it occurred to OpenAI that they’re going to need more people to do more things?
sentences.zh: 当您报道这个故事时，OpenAI 有没有想过他们需要更多的人来做更多的事情？

sentences.en: Because I look at that company and they just keep shedding all of their talent and pivoting, as you said, from one thing to the other and they’ve landed on “we need to compete with Anthropic.”
sentences.zh: 因为我观察那家公司，他们不断地放弃所有的才能，并像你所说的那样，从一件事转向另一件事，他们已经意识到“我们需要与 Anthropic 竞争”。

sentences.en: But it doesn’t seem like they can afford distraction at this scale in any way, shape, or form.
sentences.zh: 但他们似乎无法承受这种规模的任何方式、形式或形式的干扰。

blocks.html_en: They can’t. This is like the final battle. Actually, there are going to be a lot more battles, but this is a key battle in the longstanding AI arms race. This is the IPO year. Things are coming to a head. Investors are looking very closely at these companies in a way they haven’t before. They’re looking for them to turn a profit, or at least have a way to turn a profit in a way that they have not really paid too much attention to before, and it’s all coming down to the line. They cannot afford a distraction right now.

sentences.en: They can’t.
sentences.zh: 他们不能。

sentences.en: This is like the final battle.
sentences.zh: 这就像最后的战斗。

sentences.en: Actually, there are going to be a lot more battles, but this is a key battle in the longstanding AI arms race.
sentences.zh: 事实上，还会有更多的战斗，但这是长期人工智能军备竞赛中的一场关键战斗。

sentences.en: This is the IPO year.
sentences.zh: 今年是IPO年。

sentences.en: Things are coming to a head.
sentences.zh: 事情已经到了紧要关头。

sentences.en: Investors are looking very closely at these companies in a way they haven’t before.
sentences.zh: 投资者正在以前所未有的方式密切关注这些公司。

sentences.en: They’re looking for them to turn a profit, or at least have a way to turn a profit in a way that they have not really paid too much attention to before, and it’s all coming down to the line.
sentences.zh: 他们正在寻找能够盈利的方法，或者至少有一种他们以前没有真正关注过的方式来盈利，而这一切都归结为实际情况。

sentences.en: They cannot afford a distraction right now.
sentences.zh: 他们现在不能承受任何干扰。

blocks.html_en: They are reshuffling, restructuring, changing executives around. When I’ve asked them about this, they will say, “Well, it’s all been one large plan that we’ve just been doing in little pieces. It’s not like we’ve pivoted a million times, we’ve always been going towards one thing and these are just different steps along that journey.” But it doesn’t always read that way to the general public.

sentences.en: They are reshuffling, restructuring, changing executives around.
sentences.zh: 他们正在改组、重组、更换高管。

sentences.en: When I’ve asked them about this, they will say, “Well, it’s all been one large plan that we’ve just been doing in little pieces.
sentences.zh: 当我问他们这个问题时，他们会说：“好吧，这只是一个大计划，我们只是把它分成小部分来做。

sentences.en: It’s not like we’ve pivoted a million times, we’ve always been going towards one thing and these are just different steps along that journey.”
sentences.zh: 我们并不是已经转变了一百万次，我们一直在朝着一件事前进，而这些只是这一旅程中的不同步骤。”

sentences.en: But it doesn’t always read that way to the general public.
sentences.zh: 但公众并不总是这样理解。

blocks.html_en: In some ways, yes, Greg Brockman is taking control of product. That’s because Fidji Simo, the AGI chief, took leave because of her health and then she left permanently because of her health. So, it’s not all things that the company could control, but I do think there have been a lot of defections even from the rank-and-file staff. They’ve got to get more people. They’ve got to hire more, which will require even more money, and it’s not the time to get distracted by anything.

sentences.en: In some ways, yes, Greg Brockman is taking control of product.
sentences.zh: 是的，从某些方面来说，格雷格·布罗克曼正在控制产品。

sentences.en: That’s because Fidji Simo, the AGI chief, took leave because of her health and then she left permanently because of her health.
sentences.zh: 这是因为 AGI 负责人菲吉·西莫 (Fidji Simo) 因为健康原因请了假，然后又因为健康原因永久离开了。

sentences.en: So, it’s not all things that the company could control, but I do think there have been a lot of defections even from the rank-and-file staff.
sentences.zh: 所以，并不是所有事情都是公司可以控制的，但我确实认为，即使是普通员工也有很多人叛逃。

sentences.en: They’ve got to get more people.
sentences.zh: 他们必须吸引更多的人。

sentences.en: They’ve got to hire more, which will require even more money, and it’s not the time to get distracted by anything.
sentences.zh: 他们必须雇用更多的人，这将需要更多的钱，而且现在不是被任何事情分心的时候。

blocks.html_en: It’s worth noting that there’s some reporting saying OpenAI will delay its IPO. And as you and I are sitting here speaking, SpaceX’s stock price has dipped below its IPO number. These mega IPOs, they’re in some kind of danger. It might not be a good idea to do this, especially if you don’t have the revenue or, more importantly, the profits to back it up. Do you think there’s some risk that this lawsuit is the straw that pushes OpenAI to delay its IPO?

sentences.en: It’s worth noting that there’s some reporting saying OpenAI will delay its IPO.
sentences.zh: 值得注意的是，有报道称 OpenAI 将推迟 IPO。

sentences.en: And as you and I are sitting here speaking, SpaceX’s stock price has dipped below its IPO number.
sentences.zh: 当你和我坐在这里讲话时，SpaceX 的股价已跌破其 IPO 数字。

sentences.en: These mega IPOs, they’re in some kind of danger.
sentences.zh: 这些大型首次公开募股正处于某种危险之中。

sentences.en: It might not be a good idea to do this, especially if you don’t have the revenue or, more importantly, the profits to back it up.
sentences.zh: 这样做可能不是一个好主意，特别是如果您没有收入，或更重要的是，没有利润来支持它。

sentences.en: Do you think there’s some risk that this lawsuit is the straw that pushes OpenAI to delay its IPO?
sentences.zh: 您认为这起诉讼是否存在成为推动 OpenAI 推迟 IPO 的最后一根稻草的风险？

blocks.html_en: Absolutely. Reporting showed that Sam Altman was originally worried about filing its Form S-1 before Anthropic. Then it didn’t do that. Anthropic did it first and then — and this is me just speculating — it seems like after Anthropic won that part of the race, OpenAI backed off and said, “Okay, well, should we actually do this right now? We were trying really hard to win, but let’s look at the facts here.” That’s why it said, “Okay, it might be early next year, it might not be this year.”

sentences.en: Absolutely.
sentences.zh: 绝对地。

sentences.en: Reporting showed that Sam Altman was originally worried about filing its Form S-1 before Anthropic.
sentences.zh: 报道显示，Sam Altman 最初担心在 Anthropic 之前提交 S-1 表格。

sentences.en: Then it didn’t do that.
sentences.zh: 然后它就没有那么做。

sentences.en: Anthropic did it first and then — and this is me just speculating — it seems like after Anthropic won that part of the race, OpenAI backed off and said, “Okay, well, should we actually do this right now?
sentences.zh: Anthropic 首先做到了，然后——这只是我的猜测——似乎在 Anthropic 赢得了那部分比赛后，OpenAI 退缩了，说：“好吧，好吧，我们现在真的应该这样做吗？

sentences.en: We were trying really hard to win, but let’s look at the facts here.”
sentences.zh: 我们非常努力地想要获胜，但让我们看看这里的事实。”

sentences.en: That’s why it said, “Okay, it might be early next year, it might not be this year.”
sentences.zh: 这就是为什么它说：“好吧，可能是明年初，可能不是今年。”

blocks.html_en: That was before Apple’s lawsuit dropped. So now of course I’m sure OpenAI is thinking, “Okay, should we delay it even more? Especially because there’s no way we’re going to beat Anthropic to an IPO.” It seems like that might be a smart idea.

sentences.en: That was before Apple’s lawsuit dropped.
sentences.zh: 那是在苹果公司的诉讼撤销之前。

sentences.en: So now of course I’m sure OpenAI is thinking, “Okay, should we delay it even more?
sentences.zh: 所以现在我当然确信 OpenAI 正在想：“好吧，我们应该再推迟一下吗？

sentences.en: Especially because there’s no way we’re going to beat Anthropic to an IPO.”
sentences.zh: 尤其是因为我们不可能在首次公开募股方面击败 Anthropic。”

sentences.en: It seems like that might be a smart idea.
sentences.zh: 看起来这可能是一个聪明的主意。

blocks.html_en: If you can put out a great hardware device and make some big splashy profitable entry into the consumer market, that’s a good story to tell in an IPO. But if you can’t do that because everything you do is going to end up in litigation or in messy discovery on the way to litigation, maybe that all just has to be set to the side.

sentences.en: If you can put out a great hardware device and make some big splashy profitable entry into the consumer market, that’s a good story to tell in an IPO.
sentences.zh: 如果你能推出一款出色的硬件设备，并在消费市场上大赚一笔，那么这就是一个在 IPO 中讲述的好故事。

sentences.en: But if you can’t do that because everything you do is going to end up in litigation or in messy discovery on the way to litigation, maybe that all just has to be set to the side.
sentences.zh: 但如果你不能这样做，因为你所做的一切最终都会陷入诉讼或诉讼过程中的混乱发现，也许这一切都必须放在一边。

blocks.html_en: That’s pretty much my last question here. I feel like my thesis on this show and on The Verge generally has been, “Okay, I understand how this is going to work for software development and I understand how this is going to work in enterprise, although even that’s pretty shaky still. But consumers don’t love this stuff.” That there are 900 million free ChatGPT users is not a reflection of anything other than, well, Google Search got really bad and this thing will tell you it loves you.

sentences.en: That’s pretty much my last question here.
sentences.zh: 这几乎是我的最后一个问题。

sentences.en: I feel like my thesis on this show and on The Verge generally has been, “Okay, I understand how this is going to work for software development and I understand how this is going to work in enterprise, although even that’s pretty shaky still.
sentences.zh: 我觉得我在这个节目和 The Verge 上的论文总体上是：“好吧，我了解这将如何用于软件开发，我也了解这将如何在企业中发挥作用，尽管这仍然很不稳定。

sentences.en: But consumers don’t love this stuff.”
sentences.zh: 但消费者不喜欢这些东西。”

sentences.en: That there are 900 million free ChatGPT users is not a reflection of anything other than, well, Google Search got really bad and this thing will tell you it loves you.
sentences.zh: ChatGPT 有 9 亿免费用户并不能说明什么，只是 Google 搜索变得非常糟糕，而这个东西会告诉你它爱你。

blocks.html_en: But it hasn’t turned into, “We should build more data centers.” It hasn’t turned into, “We culturally love the products of AI.” It’s turned into, “Get this slop out of my face and we’re going to boo you at graduation and we’re going to come to the town board meetings and say we don’t want data centers.”

sentences.en: But it hasn’t turned into, “We should build more data centers.”
sentences.zh: 但它并没有变成“我们应该建立更多的数据中心”。

sentences.en: It hasn’t turned into, “We culturally love the products of AI.”
sentences.zh: 它并没有变成“我们在文化上喜欢人工智能产品”。

sentences.en: It’s turned into, “Get this slop out of my face and we’re going to boo you at graduation and we’re going to come to the town board meetings and say we don’t want data centers.”

blocks.html_en: • Nilay Patel

sentences.en: Nilay Patel
sentences.zh: 尼莱·帕特尔

content_html_zh:
今天，我在 Decoder 上与 The Verge 的高级 AI 记者 Hayden Field 谈论苹果和 OpenAI 之间的重大商业机密诉讼，以及这件事告诉我们 OpenAI 的未来。

到目前为止，我确信大多数 Decoder 听众都熟悉苹果公司在本案中的指控。 该公司表示，OpenAI 的一些前苹果员工在面试中针对苹果的商业机密，甚至从苹果的服务器下载了与硬件制造相关的文件。 尽管 OpenAI 否认了这些说法，但这些说法令人震惊，而且我们还没有看到该公司的法律回应。

Hayden 多年来一直在报道 OpenAI，她也刚刚与一群专门从事知识产权和商业秘密法的律师进行了交谈，以了解此案的严重性。 对于 OpenAI 来说，答案并不完全是积极的，该公司早在 2025 年就斥资 65 亿美元收购了苹果传奇设计师 Jony Ive 的人工智能硬件初创公司 io Products。

正如海登所解释的那样，苹果公司是出了名的顽固的诉讼当事人，我们拥有大量的科技行业历史来支持这一声誉。 但这些案件针对的是微软和三星这样规模的公司，这些大公司有能力承担此类诉讼的成本和干扰。

OpenAI 还不是这样的公司。 当它试图在企业和消费者市场竞争时，它仍在烧钱，并且在战略上有点偏离，并且它定期更换高管。 因此，海登和我花了很多时间讨论这个案例对于一家定义了人工智能繁荣早期的公司可能意味着什么，但最近似乎总是陷入这样或那样的危机。

好的：Verge 高级 AI 记者 Hayden Field 谈论 Apple-OpenAI 诉讼，以及 AI 行业可能因此而发生的变化。 开始了。

为了篇幅和清晰度，本次采访经过了轻微编辑。

Hayden Field，您是 The Verge 的高级人工智能记者。 欢迎回到解码器。

谢谢。 很高兴来到这里。

海登，我们将再次谈论 OpenAI。

再一次，它永远不会停止。

我们将讨论 OpenAI 能否在分心的时候保持专注，这似乎是该公司的一个主题。 虽然这一次，并不是他们自己的分心。 这是对外界的干扰，可能是由他们自己的一些行为造成的。

当然，我们谈论的是苹果商业秘密诉讼。 感觉这是一件大事。 苹果指责 OpenAI 窃取了该公司的各种信息，甚至在求职面试中也是如此——要求求职面试的人告诉他们对苹果产品的了解。 请向我们简要介绍一下这起诉讼以及这里发生的情况。

确实。 所以，这太疯狂了。 我上周写了一篇题为“山姆·奥尔特曼不需要另一场诉讼”的文章，这就是这里的情况。 从苹果公司的一些说法来看，这似乎相当令人震惊。 据称，一名前苹果员工在采访中索要商业机密，要求人们将苹果办公室外的硬件带到 OpenAI 来在采访中进行“展示和讲述”之类的事情。

有很多非常疯狂的指控。 但当我上周真正采访了一群专家时，他们说他们以前已经见过很多这样的情况了。 只是他们还没有在两名关键球员的案例中看到这一切。

如果事实证明其中一些内容是相当令人震惊的。 而且，对于该领域的专家来说，他们说：“哦，我们一直在商业秘密案件中看到这种情况。 这并不疯狂。 真正疯狂的是，这一切都集中在一个案例中，而且是两家非常大的公司。”

这真的很有趣。 这只是一种模式，也许不值得深入探讨，但在 90 年代，苹果将版权法推向了极限。 他们起诉微软涉嫌侵犯 Mac 与 Windows 的外观和感觉。 我要指出的是Windows仍然存在，微软很好。

然后在 2000 年代和 2010 年代初，苹果将专利法推向极限，起诉三星涉嫌侵犯 Android 和 Galaxy 手机的大量专利，因为苹果认为他们保护了 iPhone。 三星最终支付了约 10 亿美元的损失，而这些案件就这样一直循环下去。

但我要指出的是，Android 仍然存在，三星做得很好。 苹果可能围绕3G和4G许可重新定义了专利法，但从根本上说，它实际上并没有成功地通过知识产权诉讼阻止竞争对手。

所以，你从版权到专利，现在又到商业秘密，这是第三大知识产权，苹果公司通过 OpenAI 再次做到了这一点。 我的一个大问题是，OpenAI 能否像微软和三星那样支付这笔钱。 如果你看看三星，花费 10 亿美元的罚款来让 Galaxy 手机起步并成为移动领域的主要竞争对手，这可能是该公司有史以来最简单、最好的投资。

但我不知道 OpenAI 在同一时刻是否是同一家公司。 当然，所有转向 OpenAI 的苹果员工似乎都超出了极限，至少从苹果现在告诉我们的故事来看是这样。

阅读诉讼中最疯狂的主张以及正在交换的信息真是太疯狂了。 据称，其中一人在设备上访问了苹果的机密信息，然后说：“哈哈！ 太有趣了，我可以访问这个。” 那里有很多。

我一直在想同样的事情。 这实际上取决于苹果是否愿意变得小气或超级逻辑。 当我就此采访了一群律师时，他们说苹果公司是出了名的顽固的诉讼当事人，正如我们在过去的案件中看到的那样。 而且，他们确实会让事情走得更远。 他们有很多钱并且愿意使用它。 这实际上取决于他们有多疯狂。

我认为，如果 OpenAI 支付给他们足够的钱，他们可能会达成和解，但是 OpenAI 能够做到吗？ 他们已经度过了非常艰难的一年。 他们即将上市，所有投资者都在关注他们的底线，想知道他们什么时候才能盈利。 现在是向苹果公司投入大量资金的艰难时期。 我们必须看看他们会做什么。

我认为这将进入审判阶段，并且是一个非常漫长的长达数年的事情。 我采访过的很多律师也是这么说的。 但我可以看到一个世界，他们只是想出一种方法来聚集所有这些钱并支付，也许苹果会接受它。

在三星专利案件中，它们永远持续下去。 期间，蒂姆·库克正在参加一个代码会议，我问他：“你在做什么？ 这有什么成就吗？” 他非常严肃地看着我，说道：“嗯，我们认为标准必要专利的许可费用不正常。” 我当时想，“这不是你做这一切的原因。 这不是重点，重点是你很生气，你想要让这起诉讼落空，而不管名义上的原因是什么，你都会让这起诉讼落空。”

这感觉就像是这里的一大难题。 OpenAI 挖走了一批苹果员工。 众所周知，苹果最著名的设计师 Jony Ive 正在与 OpenAI 合作开发硬件设备。 唐探，OpenAI 首席硬件官。 他在苹果公司享有“行事快速、松散”的名声，这是彭博社马克·古尔曼 (Mark Gurman) 说的。

他似乎是故事的中心人物，与其说是乔尼·伊维，不如说是唐探。 而那首由一群前苹果员工演奏的曲子可能演奏得太快、太松散，几乎感觉像是个人的，几乎感觉涉及到很多情感。 谈论唐探，谈论所有这些人被指控所做的事情，感觉这比标准的大公司纠纷更加个人化。

关于你所说的与蒂姆·库克的谈话，我确实相信人们低估了公司的渺小。 近年来我们已经看到了这种情况，比如 OpenAI 与埃隆·马斯克的诉讼。 如果公司不试图变得琐碎的话，很多事情是可以避免的，这很好。 他们可以做到这一点，他们有足够的钱来做到这一点。 但人们有时低估了它在这些事情中所起的作用。

至于唐探，是的，他在苹果工作了24年。 他是 Apple Watch 的副总裁。 他于 2024 年离开，前往 Jony Ive 的硬件公司 io Products 工作，显然 OpenAI 最终收购了该公司。

OpenAI 随后任命他为首席硬件官。 因此，他在 OpenAI 担任首席高管，但这是在 Apple 工作了 24 年后。 根据苹果公司的诉讼声称，他是许多商业秘密收集的策划者，涉及他将进行的采访并询问秘密代号项目。 然后接受采访的人会觉得也许他们能够告诉他一些事情，因为，哦，好吧，如果他也不参与的话，为什么他会知道这个项目的名称或有关它的这些细节呢？ 因此，据称，他们觉得自己可以更自由地说话。

还有几次，他问：“嘿，你为什么不把一些电池或其他硬件带到你过去工作过的苹果办公室外面，这样我们就可以做一个展示和讲述呢？” 仔细研究这些说法非常有趣。 看起来他真的并不担心如果这些说法属实的话会发生什么。

这里的另一个主要人物是乔尼·艾维（Jony Ive），他是苹果公司非常著名的前设计师，曾与史蒂夫·乔布斯（Steve Jobs）和所有这些著名的项目一起工作。 据报道，我想说，我与苹果的关系非常紧张。

您是否觉得更广泛的人工智能行业正在关注苹果公司的迟到？ 因为我听过很多这样的说法，这只是苹果试图减缓消费者面临的最大威胁，因为它推出了 Apple Intelligence，并试图迎头赶上。

这绝对是其中的一部分。 大家都知道，苹果在人工智能方面的落后是出了名的，多年来，这一直是人们谈论的一个大话题，也是该公司的尴尬之处。 所以，这肯定是其中的一部分。

但这起诉讼并不是关于有人为 Siri“窃取商业机密”。 这是关于硬件的，特别是“展示和讲述”硬件之类的事情。 苹果在硬件方面做得非常出色，而且从各方面来看，他们几乎都是最擅长的。

所以，我认为有些人正在这么想。 如果苹果能够以任何方式阻止竞争对手取得领先，这对苹果来说是一件好事。 但我认为，这是一起专门针对硬件的商业秘密诉讼，这一事实已经推翻了其中一些主张；他们并不是想声称 OpenAI 正在窃取 Siri AI 的秘密，而 Siri 可能并不拥有很多秘密。

[笑]他们就像是，“请利用我们所知道的关于 Siri AI 的一切。 那真的会让你跪倒在地。” 苹果可以承受这种干扰，你已经在这次谈话中提到了这一点。 苹果可以雇佣一群律师，让他们按照自己的意愿顽强地坚持下去。 也许苹果的其他部门甚至不会注意到这种情况正在发生，因为他们正忙于制造下一代 iPhone。

不管苹果花了多少年时间和大约 100 亿美元试图制造一辆汽车，但都没有成功。 苹果的其他部门甚至没有想到正在发生这样的事情。 他们只是继续运送手机和笔记本电脑，一切都很好。

OpenAI 不是那家公司。 OpenAI 甚至可能没有足够的高管来承担另一次干扰的成本。 他们不断离开。 OpenAI 总裁格雷格·布罗克曼 (Greg Brockman) 似乎亲自运营着整个公司。

当您报道这个故事时，OpenAI 有没有想过他们需要更多的人来做更多的事情？ 因为我观察那家公司，他们不断地放弃所有的才能，并像你所说的那样，从一件事转向另一件事，他们已经意识到“我们需要与 Anthropic 竞争”。 但他们似乎无法承受这种规模的任何方式、形式或形式的干扰。

他们不能。 这就像最后的战斗。 事实上，还会有更多的战斗，但这是长期人工智能军备竞赛中的一场关键战斗。 今年是IPO年。 事情已经到了紧要关头。 投资者正在以前所未有的方式密切关注这些公司。 他们正在寻找能够盈利的方法，或者至少有一种他们以前没有真正关注过的方式来盈利，而这一切都归结为实际情况。 他们现在不能承受任何干扰。

他们正在改组、重组、更换高管。 当我问他们这个问题时，他们会说：“好吧，这只是一个大计划，我们只是把它分成小部分来做。 我们并不是已经转变了一百万次，我们一直在朝着一件事前进，而这些只是这一旅程中的不同步骤。” 但公众并不总是这样理解。

是的，从某些方面来说，格雷格·布罗克曼正在控制产品。 这是因为 AGI 负责人菲吉·西莫 (Fidji Simo) 因为健康原因请了假，然后又因为健康原因永久离开了。 所以，并不是所有事情都是公司可以控制的，但我确实认为，即使是普通员工也有很多人叛逃。 他们必须吸引更多的人。 他们必须雇用更多的人，这将需要更多的钱，而且现在不是被任何事情分心的时候。

值得注意的是，有报道称 OpenAI 将推迟 IPO。 当你和我坐在这里讲话时，SpaceX 的股价已跌破其 IPO 数字。 这些大型首次公开募股正处于某种危险之中。 这样做可能不是一个好主意，特别是如果您没有收入，或更重要的是，没有利润来支持它。 您认为这起诉讼是否存在成为推动 OpenAI 推迟 IPO 的最后一根稻草的风险？

绝对地。 报道显示，Sam Altman 最初担心在 Anthropic 之前提交 S-1 表格。 然后它就没有那么做。 Anthropic 首先做到了，然后——这只是我的猜测——似乎在 Anthropic 赢得了那部分比赛后，OpenAI 退缩了，说：“好吧，好吧，我们现在真的应该这样做吗？ 我们非常努力地想要获胜，但让我们看看这里的事实。” 这就是为什么它说：“好吧，可能是明年初，可能不是今年。”

那是在苹果公司的诉讼撤销之前。 所以现在我当然确信 OpenAI 正在想：“好吧，我们应该再推迟一下吗？ 尤其是因为我们不可能在首次公开募股方面击败 Anthropic。” 看起来这可能是一个聪明的主意。

如果你能推出一款出色的硬件设备，并在消费市场上大赚一笔，那么这就是一个在 IPO 中讲述的好故事。 但如果你不能这样做，因为你所做的一切最终都会陷入诉讼或诉讼过程中的混乱发现，也许这一切都必须放在一边。

这几乎是我的最后一个问题。 我觉得我在这个节目和 The Verge 上的论文总体上是：“好吧，我了解这将如何用于软件开发，我也了解这将如何在企业中发挥作用，尽管这仍然很不稳定。 但消费者不喜欢这些东西。” ChatGPT 有 9 亿免费用户并不能说明什么，只是 Google 搜索变得非常糟糕，而这个东西会告诉你它爱你。

但它并没有变成“我们应该建立更多的数据中心”。 它并没有变成“我们在文化上喜欢人工智能产品”。 它变成了，“把这句废话从我脸上拿走，我们会在毕业典礼上嘘你，我们会参加镇议会会议并说我们不想要数据中心。”

那里有一些巨大的差距。 您认为 OpenAI 可以缩小这一差距吗？ 因为一直以来都是公司追逐大消费产品愿景、大消费市场。 如果它无法缩小差距，就应该关闭它并与 Anthropic 竞争企业用途。 我不知道会是哪一个。 通过所有这些报道，您是否觉得 OpenAI 已经意识到消费者方面的争论有多么重要？

我认为他们在一定程度上知道，但不是全部。 他们会通过演讲、研究论文和推文来了解这一点。 他们有一些公关路线，他们会承认这是一件事，但我认为他们没有意识到这个裂缝有多深。 差距这么大。 我不认为他们是唯一一家关闭它的公司——如果它真的要关闭的话。 当谈到人工智能时，人们非常担心很多事情，并且对此非常情绪化。

这不仅仅是逻辑。 我认为，如果这只是一个逻辑差距——有些人从逻辑上认为这不好，有些人从逻辑上认为这是好的——我认为他们可能可以弥补这一差距。 但当情绪发挥作用时，不，我认为他们无法完全弥合这种差距。

对我来说，六年前当我成为一名人工智能记者时，我的朋友没有一个真正了解或关心我写的内容。 那很好。 无论如何，这确实是利基市场。 从四年前开始，它就会出现在酒吧、俱乐部。 我走在街上，有人问我这件事，但他们不知道我是一名人工智能记者。 所以，当他们得知我是一名人工智能记者时，这种情况就会发生得更多。 但只是随机地，这会出现在人们身上并使他们变得非常情绪化。 所以，是的，我只是不认为 OpenAI 会成为弥合这一差距的公司。

我们将看看这一切的硬件会发生什么。 我见过的很多人——TikTok 的创作者、其他平台的创作者——都在试图改变这里的叙述，他们说：“嘿，不要被抛在后面。 学习如何使用这些东西。” 这可能是有效的，但他们很多时候构建它的方式更多的是，“哦，这是一个很高的学习曲线。 在它抛弃你之前，你必须学会​​如何使用它。”

这并不是一个很高的学习曲线，这取决于你如何使用它，但你知道我的意思。 有时候，是的，如果你使用 Codex 并进行各种激烈的氛围编码，是的，当然是这样。 但如果你只是与聊天机器人聊天，那么学习曲线确实不高。

我个人认为所有算法社交视频内容都是一场人造草皮活动。 这就是我。 这是一个阴谋论，我们将其放在一边。 我确实想向您推荐一个名为“Hayden in the Club”的视频系列，您只需去俱乐部与人们谈论人工智能即可。

[笑]我很乐意正式地这样做，而不是一直让这种事非正式地发生在我身上。

我已经很久没来俱乐部了。 我年轻时俱乐部可不是这样的。 事情已经改变了。

海登，这太棒了。 我强烈怀疑今年剩下的时间你会回到这个节目谈论这起诉讼和 OpenAI。 我们很快就会让你回来。

等不及了。 谢谢。
